one_liner: GOOG +0.58% to $353 — volume drying up to 0.82x after the June 26 selling climax (82.5M) and June 29 AR (25.6M); the SC-AR-ST pattern is executing textbook Wyckoff; accumulate signal pending if Secondary Test holds.

price_action.intraday_story: GOOG opened flat, ranged narrowly between $348.11 and $355.39 on declining volume (21M, 0.82x avg). Three-session sequence after the June 26 SC: SC (82.5M, 3.22x, close $334.69) → AR (25.6M, 1.00x, close $351.28, +5.0%) → today's quiet drift (21.0M, 0.82x, close $353.33, +0.58%). The volume dry-up on the second day of the AR is exactly what Wyckoff methodology predicts — the AR exhausts itself, and price begins to drift back for the Secondary Test.

news_catalyst.headline: GOOG Consolidating Post-Dow Jones Inclusion — Earnings July 23 is Next Catalyst
news_catalyst.summary: No new material news today. GOOG is consolidating the +5% Dow Jones inclusion bounce from June 29. The mechanical index-fund buying from the DJIA addition has been absorbed. The next major catalyst is Q2 earnings on July 23, where the market will scrutinize Gemini growth vs. Claude/GPT-4o competitive positioning, capex trajectory (currently $146.4B investment spend), and Search ad revenue amid AI disruption concerns. AI talent departures (John Jumper, Noam Shazeer) remain a background headwind. The stock is up 5.4% from the June 26 selling climax at $334.69 — well within the typical 5–8% Automatic Rally range after a SC event.

technical.summary:
The Wyckoff SC-AR pattern is executing with textbook precision. Volume sequence: SC (82.5M) → AR (25.6M) → today (21.0M). Each day's volume is lower than the previous while price holds near the AR high ($351–$355) — this is the 'volume dry-up at AR highs' that precedes the Secondary Test in Wyckoff accumulation Phase A-B.

RSI at 46 is below neutral but recovering from the June 26 extreme of 27.95. MACD is still slightly bearish but improving. EMA50 at $369.34 and EMA200 at $379.82 are both overhead — the full trend reversal requires significant work.

The next expected event: a pullback from the $351–$355 AR zone toward the $334–$342 range (the SC low and immediate post-SC consolidation). If that pullback occurs on below-average volume (below 20M) and holds above $333.69, that's the Secondary Test (ST) confirmation. Once the ST is confirmed, the accumulate signal fires.

wyckoff_narrative:
The June 26 session printed a Selling Climax by the updated algorithm definition: 82.5M shares (3.22x avg), close of $334.69 below bar midpoint ($338.90). June 29's +5% AR on exactly 1.00x average volume ($25.6M) was the Automatic Rally. Today's quiet drift ($21M, 0.82x) on negligible price change (+0.58%) is what Wyckoff calls the 'backing up' — price consolidating near the AR highs, supply and demand temporarily balanced.

The next stage is the Secondary Test (ST): a pullback toward the SC zone ($334–$342) that should occur on volume materially below the SC's 82.5M — ideally below 25M. If GOOG dips to $338–$345 on 15–20M shares and then holds, the SC-AR-ST sequence is complete and Phase B accumulation begins. That's when the accumulate signal is appropriate.

The July 23 earnings creates a timeline pressure: the SC-AR-ST sequence typically plays out over 1–3 weeks. If the Secondary Test completes before July 23, we'd enter with earnings as a binary catalyst. If not, waiting for post-earnings clarity may be the better risk management approach.

valuation.summary: DCF base case at $411 — 14.1% upside from current $353.33. Grade A fundamentals (0.82 score) with 82% earnings growth YoY. The margin of safety is present but modest at the current price; it was more compelling at the $334.69 SC low (18.6% upside). A Secondary Test pullback to $338–$345 would restore the margin of safety to ~20%.

key_strengths: Analyst consensus Strong Buy, $426 mean target
Dow Jones inclusion provides structural passive buying floor
key_concerns: OBV deeply negative — sustained distribution since mid-June
AI talent departures — Gemini competitive risk
Earnings July 23 is binary: capex trajectory + Gemini growth scrutiny
fundamentals.summary: Grade A fundamentals support the SC-AR-ST thesis. The stock is not overvalued; it is testing support after a sentiment-driven selloff. Wait for the Secondary Test to complete before upgrading to accumulate.

risks: Secondary Test pullback to $334–$342 is expected — if it fails to hold (close below $333.69 on expanding volume), the accumulation thesis is invalidated
July 23 earnings binary: any capex guidance acceleration or Gemini revenue miss breaks the recovery
Volume dry-up is positive but OBV at -246M means the cumulative selling pressure hasn't reversed yet
